volumeTitle: Casina. The Casket Comedy. Curculio. Epidicus. The Two Menaechmuses
label: The Casket Comedy (Section 2.3.596 - Section 4.1.668b)

sections.label: Section 2.3.596
sections.translation: I trust in the Gods and in yourself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.596b
sections.translation: And I in the same .— that you ’ ll now go home .

sections.label: Section 2.3.597
sections.translation: Young man , stay and listen .

sections.label: Section 2.3.597b
sections.translation: What , are you calling to me , woman ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.598
sections.translation: To you .

sections.label: Section 2.3.598b
sections.original: Quid negoti est ? nam occupatus sum ampliter .
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ? For I ’ m fully engaged .

sections.label: Section 2.3.599
sections.translation: Who lives there ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.599b
sections.translation: Demipho , my master .

sections.label: Section 2.3.600
sections.original: Nempe istic est , qui Alcesimarcho filiam
sections.translation: It is he , I suppose , that has betrothed his daughter with such great wealth to Alcesimarchus ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.602
sections.translation: It is he himself .

sections.label: Section 2.3.602b
sections.original: Eho tu , quam vos igitur filiam
sections.translation: How now , you ? What other daughter , then , are you people now in search of ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.603b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you ; not his daughter by his wife , but his wife ’ s daughter .

sections.label: Section 2.3.605
sections.translation: What ’ s the meaning of that speech ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.605b
sections.translation: By a former woman , I say , my master had a daughter born .

sections.label: Section 2.3.606b
sections.translation: Surely , just now you said you were in search of the daughter of her who has been talking here .

sections.label: Section 2.3.608
sections.translation: Her daughter I am in search of .

sections.label: Section 2.3.608b
sections.translation: In what way then , pray , is she a former woman , who is now his wife ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.609b
sections.translation: Woman , whoever you are , you weary me with your prating .

sections.label: Section 2.3.611
sections.original: medioxumam quam duxit uxorem , ex ea
sections.translation: The middle woman [*] whom he had for a wife , of her this maiden was born that ’ s being given to Alcesimarchus . That wife is dead . Do you understand now ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.613b
sections.translation: I understand that quite well ; but it ’ s this knotty point I ’ m enquiring about , how

sections.label: Section 2.3.615
sections.original: prior pósterior sit et posterior sit prior ?
sections.translation: the first can be the last , the last be the first .

sections.label: Section 2.3.616
sections.original: Prius hanc compressit quam uxorem duxit domum ,
sections.translation: The fact is this ; this woman he ravished before he took her home as his wife ; before that she was pregnant , and before that she gave birth to a daughter : after she gave birth to her , she ordered the infant to be exposed ; I myself exposed her ; another woman took her away ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.620
sections.original: ego inspectavi . érus hanc duxit postibi .
sections.translation: I was on the look-out ; after that , my master married her . That girl , her daughter , we are now in search of . ( MELAENIS turns aside her head .) Why now , with face upturned , are you looking up towards the heavens ?

sections.label: Section 2.3.623
sections.original: Ei nunciam istuc quo properabas , nil moror .
sections.translation: Now , then , be off at once whither you were hastening ; I won ’ t detain you ; I understand it now .

sections.label: Section 2.3.624b
sections.translation: I ’ troth , to the Deities I do give thanks ;

sections.label: Section 2.3.625
sections.original: nam ni intellexes , numquam , credo , amitteres .—
sections.translation: for if you hadn ’ t understood me , I do think you would never have let me go .

sections.label: Section 2.3.626
sections.original: Nunc mihi bonae necessumst esse ingratiis ,
sections.translation: Now it ’ s necessary for me to be honest , whether I will or no , although I had rather not ; I find the thing is discovered . Now will I myself lay them under an obligation to me , rather than she shall peach upon me . I ’ ll go home ,

sections.label: Section 2.3.630
sections.translation: and I ’ ll bring Silenium to her parents .

sections.label: Section 3.1.631
sections.original: Rem élocuta sum tibi omnem ; sequere hac me , Selenium ,
sections.translation: I ’ ve disclosed the whole matter to you ; follow , my Silenium , that you may rather belong to those to whom you ought to belong , than be mine . Although against my will I shall part with you , still I ’ ll reconcile my mind to consult that which in especial conduces to your benefit . ( Giving her a casket .)

sections.label: Section 3.1.635
sections.original: nam hic crepundia insunt , quibuscum te illa olim ad me detulit ,
sections.translation: For here in this are the trinkets [*] , together with which she who gave you to me formerly brought you to me ; that your parents may recognize you the more easily . Take this casket , Halisca , and then go and knock at that door ( pointing to the house of DEMIPHO ) : say that I request that some one will come from within . Make haste , quickly .

sections.label: Section 3.1.639
sections.original: Recipe me ad te , Mors , amicum et benevolum .
sections.translation: Death , receive me unto thyself , a friend and well-wisher to me !

sections.label: Section 3.1.639_640
sections.translation: My mother , to our sorrow , we are undone !

sections.label: Section 3.1.641b
sections.original: Vtrum hac me feriam an ab laeva latus ?
sections.translation: Whether shall I pierce my side here ( striking his right side ) or on the left .

sections.label: Section 3.1.642
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter with you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.642b
sections.translation: Don ’ t you see Alcesimarchus ? He ’ s grasping a sword .

sections.label: Section 3.1.643
sections.translation: What art about ? Thou art delaying . Quit the light of day .

sections.label: Section 3.1.643b
sections.translation: Do run and aid him , pray , that he mayn ’ t kill himself .

sections.label: Section 3.1.644b
sections.translation: O Safety more healthful than my own safety , you now , whether I wish or don ’ t wish , alone do cause me to live .

sections.label: Section 3.1.646
sections.translation: Fie on it ! Were you ready to commit such violence ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.646b
sections.translation: I ’ ve nought to do with you — to you I ’ m dead . ( Clasping SILENIUM in his arms .) Her , as I hold her , I ’ m determined not to lose . For , by heaven , I am resolved henceforth to have her entirely rivetted fast unto me . ( Goes to the door of the house , and calls .) Where are you , servants ? Shut the door with bolts , with bars ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.650
sections.original: ilico : hanc ego tetulero intra limen .—
sections.translation: when I shall have carried her within the threshold !

sections.label: Section 3.1.650b
sections.translation: He ’ s gone off : he has carried the damsel away . I ’ ll go — I ’ ll at once follow him in-doors , that he may know of me these same things , if from being angered with me I can render him pleased .

sections.label: Section 4.1.653
sections.original: Nullam ego me vidisse credo magis anum excruciabilem
sections.translation: I do believe I never saw a more tormenting old nag than this is . What she just now confessed to me , is she to be denying it ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.655
sections.original: sed eccam eram video . sed quíd hoc est , haec quod cistella hic iacet
sections.translation: But look , I see my mistress . Why ( seeing the casket on the ground ) , how ’ s this , that this casket is lying here with these trinkets , and that I see no other person in the street ? I must act the child ’ s part [*] ; I ’ ll stoop to pick up the casket .

sections.label: Section 4.1.658
sections.translation: What are you about , Lampadio ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.658b
sections.original: Haec cistella numnam hinc ab nobis domo est ?
sections.translation: Is this casket from out of our house here , I wonder . For I picked it up , lying here near the door .

sections.label: Section 4.1.659b
sections.translation: What news do you bring about the old woman ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.660b
sections.original: Scelestiorem in terra nullam esse alteram .
sections.translation: That there ’ s not one other on earth more wicked . She denies all those things which she just now confessed to me . But , i ’ faith , for me to allow that old jade to be laughing at me , it ’ s preferable for me to die by any kind of death .

sections.label: Section 4.1.663b
sections.translation: Ye Gods , I do adjure you by our trust in you !

sections.label: Section 4.1.664
sections.translation: Why do you call upon the Gods ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.664b
sections.translation: Save us !

sections.label: Section 4.1.664c
sections.translation: What ’ s the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.664d
sections.translation: These are the trinkets with which you exposed my little daughter to death .

sections.label: Section 4.1.666
sections.translation: Are you in your senses ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.666b
sections.translation: These certainly are .

sections.label: Section 4.1.666c
sections.translation: Do you persist ?

sections.label: Section 4.1.666d
sections.translation: These are they .

sections.label: Section 4.1.666e
sections.translation: If any other woman were to speak to me after that fashion , I should say she was drunk .

sections.label: Section 4.1.668
sections.translation: By heaven ! I ’ m talking no nonsense .

sections.label: Section 4.1.668b
sections.translation: But prithee , whence in the world did these come , or what Deity placed this before our door ? As though
